behavior_summary: Implements pentagram-related gameplay: cleaning up improper/extra items when someone enters the pentagram trigger area (U8P_PENT::enterFastArea), reacting to physical hits on the pentagram to start ritual effects (gotHit), and handling player 'use' attempts to activate the pentagram ritual (use).

U8P_PENT::enterFastArea: when the fast-area trigger is entered and the global flag acolyteKilled is set and the triggering item's map/frame match expected values, the handler enumerates nearby items and destroys those that match specific frame/shape masks and positional constraints — effectively pruning or enforcing the pentagram's center contents.

gotHit: validates hit parameters and global state (tonguing, pyrosFree, etc.), selects a valid focus/candle item, sets a 'tonguing' ritual flag, may put the avatar into stasis, spawns multiple asynchronous visual/effect processes (FREE::* and METHOD::*), and manipulates or destroys involved items to start the ritual sequence.

use: validates party/quest prerequisites (vardionJoined, baneJoined, acolyteKilled), checks for a single valid focus at the pentagram center and an appropriate set of lit candles, barks feedback to the player when requirements fail, and when validated spawns the ritual/effect processes and calls internal helpers to build/validate item lists and orchestrate the activation.
notable_control_flow: Fast-area entry path is gated by the global flag acolyteKilled and by the triggering item's map/frame bits; if either fails, U8P_PENT::enterFastArea exits without altering the world.
Item enumeration loops test item frames/types and spatial X/Y ranges relative to the trigger; matching items are destroyed via Item::destroy.

gotHit uses multiple guards (tonguing flag, pyrosFree, hit parameters, item frame/type) to prevent duplicate or invalid activations; when conditions pass it sets tongue-related state and spawns multiple asynchronous routines.

use performs layered validation: quest/party flags first (vardionJoined, baneJoined, sponsorTest affects messaging), then presence and uniqueness of a center focus, then count/state of candles; failure at any step produces a player-facing bark and aborts activation.
open_questions: Exact numeric frame/shape mask values and which concrete in-game item types (e.g., 'focus' vs 'candle') correspond to each frame test — inference is based on frame/shape checks but not explicit names.
Precise side effects and durations of the spawned FREE::* and METHOD::* processes (visuals, damage, NPC behavior) — disassembly shows spawns but not the high-level gameplay effects.
Where and when the 'tonguing' flag is cleared after gotHit sets it — clearing sequences are inferred but not present in this snippet.
Exact player-facing strings used by PENT::use failure branches (bark text is invoked but literal strings are not fully recovered here).

main_function.name: U8P_PENT::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: On fast-area (trigger) entry, ensure the pentagram center contains only allowed items by inspecting the triggering actor/item and removing nearby items that do not match the pentagram's expected frame/shape and spatial constraints. Acts as cleanup/enforcement when the acolyte-related state applies.
entry_conditions: Proceeds only if global flag 'acolyteKilled' is non-zero.
Proceeds only when the triggering item's map and frame bits match expected values (ensures correct pentagram variant/state).

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger-area handler that prunes incorrect or unwanted items within the pentagram center when conditions (acolyteKilled and trigger-item frame/map) are met.

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::gotHit
function_map.role: Handles Event 6 (pentagram being struck): validates hit and global state, may set ritual state (tonguing), spawn visual/effect processes, set avatar stasis, and manipulate/destroy selected items to begin a ritual sequence.

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::use
function_map.role: Handles 'use' (player activation): verifies party/quest preconditions, checks for a single valid focus and proper candles/lights, barks player messages on failure, and on success orchestrates the ritual by spawning effects and calling internal list/validation helpers.

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::5336
function_map.role: Internal validation helper (inferred): used by use/gotHit to validate item lists or ritual preconditions.

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::3940
function_map.role: Internal helper (inferred): possibly performs a different validation pass or state check for activation sequences.

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::39E7
function_map.role: Internal helper (inferred): builds/filters nearby item lists (foci/candles) for ritual operations.

function_map.name: U8P_PENT::3B43
function_map.role: Internal helper (inferred): further list manipulation or ritual orchestration utility used during use/gotHit.

internal_calls.purpose: Validate ritual prerequisites or an item list prior to activation.
Additional validation or alternate path checks during use; exact role inferred from invocation context.
Build or filter a nearby-item list (candles/foci) used to decide whether activation can proceed.
Post-validation list handling or item-movement helper invoked as part of orchestration.

external_calls.purpose: Check the triggering item's map/region to gate fast-area behavior to the correct map.
Inspect triggering-item frame bits to determine variant/type and whether cleanup should run.
Obtain item X coordinate for spatial inclusion/exclusion tests when enumerating nearby items.
Obtain item Y coordinate for spatial inclusion/exclusion tests.
Remove items that do not comply with pentagram layout or that must be pruned when the avatar enters the center.
Start asynchronous visual/effect processes (addresses in FREE::* or METHOD::*) to present ritual visuals or run concurrent logic.
Check the struck item's frame/type to decide if it is a valid focus/candle for initiating the tonguing ritual.
Query whether the avatar is already in stasis before setting it for the ritual sequence.
Set avatar stasis to prevent player movement/actions during ritual visual sequences.
Provide player-facing messages when ritual prerequisites fail or to narrate stages of activation.
Check NPC busy state before spawning additional processes that act on or near NPCs.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Gates pentagram behavior variant; when set allows U8P_PENT::enterFastArea pruning and choice of activation branches in use/gotHit.

reads.name: tonguing
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether a 'tonguing' ritual sequence is already active; read to prevent redundant activations in gotHit.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used to gate whether certain pyrotechnic/free effects may be spawned during gotHit.

reads.name: vardionJoined
reads.likely_effect: Used during use to require specific party membership before allowing ritual activation or to choose an alternative message.

reads.name: baneJoined
reads.likely_effect: Also used in use to check party composition prerequisites for activation.

reads.name: sponsorTest
reads.likely_effect: A boolean that changes validation/messaging behavior in use (affects which bark or path is chosen).

writes.name: tonguing
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when gotHit successfully chooses a valid focus and begins the tonguing ritual; prevents reentry until cleared elsewhere.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements pentagram-related gameplay: cleaning up improper/extra items when someone enters the pentagram trigger area (U8P_PENT::enterFastArea), reacting to physical hits on the pentagram to start ritual effects (gotHit), and handling player 'use' attempts to activate the pentagram ritual (use).
